subject: [PATCH v5 01/36] mm: refactor find_next_best_node to find_next_best_node_in

2026-07-21.patch_summary: This patch refactors the find_next_best_node function to make it more flexible by allowing it to take an explicit list of candidates instead of just the N_MEMORY set. This change enables building fallback lists that include non-N_MEMORY nodes, but does not introduce any functional changes as all current callers still select from N_MEMORY. The refactor involves renaming the function to find_next_best_node_in and updating several calls in memory-tiers.c and page_alloc.c to use the new function.
